create.title: Create ingress
create.transcodeHint: Required for RTMP/URL; optional for WHIP.

list.title: Active ingresses
list.subtitle: Existing publishing endpoints. The eye reveals each one's ingest URL and stream key.
list.searchPlaceholder: Search by id, name or room…
list.empty: No ingresses yet. Create your first publishing endpoint.

reveal.title: Ingest credentials
reveal.passwordRequired: This ingress also requires the stream password issued when it was created.
reveal.help: Paste the Server and Stream Key into your encoder (OBS, camera, etc.). Treat the key as a secret.

create.roomHint: Required. One room per camera (e.g. cam1).
create.infoHint: The device sends JPEG frames over wss:// (1 binary message = 1 frame). Playback is sub-second MJPEG on the same /play URL. Ideal for ESP32-CAM / CCTV.
create.error: Could not create the WebSocket ingest key.

details.streamKey: Stream key (wsk_) — send as Authorization: Bearer
details.help: Flash the ESP32 with the example firmware (streamhub-docs/integrations/esp32cam_ws_ingest.ino), set the WebSocket URL and the key, and the camera shows up in Streams and on /play.

list.title: WebSocket cameras (ESP32/MJPEG)
list.subtitle: Keys for direct WS ingest. Active = the camera is connected and sending frames right now.
list.loadError: Could not load the WebSocket cameras.
list.empty: No WebSocket cameras yet. Create one with the "WebSocket (ESP32/MJPEG)" ingest type above.
list.confirmRemove: Revoke key {{id}}? An active camera will be disconnected immediately.

reveal.title: WebSocket camera credentials
reveal.help: The device opens the WebSocket URL and authenticates with `Authorization: Bearer <key>` (or `&key=` in the URL). Each binary message is one JPEG frame.
